personality: 7 Dark-First Utility DARK with Camera Glassmorphism
personality_one_liner: A high-contrast, tactical pro-creator tool merging immersive Snapchat-style camera chrome with precise DAW waveform timeline control.

spacing.rule: Keep timeline and camera chrome dense and out of the way. Use generous padding for the record button hit area.

do's: Use heavy frosted glass (blur + >=75% tint opacity) for the top DAW waveform container
Use glass for the right-side vertical camera tool strip
Use glass for the double-tap popover menu on clips
dont's: Do not blur the actual waveform or recorded clip chips on the timeline
Do not use glass on the Projects List utility cards

screens.name: Projects List
screens.purpose: A utility-focused dashboard displaying existing music video drafts and a prominent FAB to start a new project by uploading an MP3.

states.loading: Centered spinner with a tactical 'Loading Projects...' label
states.empty: Illustration of a music timeline + 'Upload MP3 to start' primary CTA

screens.layout: Standard vertical list layout. Each project card features a left-aligned video thumbnail, project title, and duration in Barlow Condensed. A floating action button (brandPrimary) sits at the bottom right for new projects. No glassmorphism used here.

screens.name: Camera Studio
screens.purpose: The primary creation interface merging a full-bleed camera viewfinder with a top-anchored DAW waveform timeline to record synced clips over uploaded music.

states.loading: Full dark screen with a pulsing record icon
states.empty: N/A - camera feed is always live
states.error: Toast notification over the camera feed

screens.layout: Full-bleed background (simulated camera feed). CRITICAL: Apply top and bottom vertical gradient scrims to ensure text readability over the feed. Top area: A wide glass container holding the horizontal MP3 waveform, a vertical 'door' scrubber/playhead (brandPrimary), and overlaid clip segments (brandTertiary chips). Right edge: Vertical stack of glass pill icons (flip, flash, import). Bottom center: A massive Snapchat-style circular record button. Double-tapping a timeline clip opens a small glass contextual menu (Delete, Split, Revert).

screens.name: Preview and Export
screens.purpose: Playback of the compiled music video with timeline clips synced to the MP3, followed by a final export action.
screens.layout: Full-bleed video player. Minimal UI chrome to let the content shine. A sticky, full-width glass bottom sheet containing the 'Export Video' primary CTA.

camera_viewfinder_mock.alt: Woman singing acting as the live camera viewfinder feed

instructions_to_main_agent: Build the Camera Studio as a full-bleed absolute layout. Use 'camera_viewfinder_mock' as the flex:1 background to simulate the live feed.

CRITICAL: Insert top-to-bottom and bottom-to-top gradient scrims (transparent to ≥75% opacity dark) over the camera feed behind the top timeline and bottom record button. DO NOT render text directly on the image without it.

Top Timeline UI: Needs a horizontal scrollable view containing the waveform. Use a vertical scrubber line ('door') positioned absolutely to act as the playhead. Recorded clips should be absolute positioned 'chips' over the waveform track.
Snapchat-Style Record Button: Place it bottom center. Needs to be an oversized ring that fills/pulses when actively recording. Do not use standard buttons for this.
Double-Tap Menu: Wrap the timeline clips in GestureDetector or Pressable (with onDoublePress logic). Render a small glass contextual menu near it with 'Delete' and 'Revert'.
Strictly follow the Dark-First Utility palette. No blues, purples, or indigo hues anywhere. Use 'brandPrimary' (Signal Red) for all active recording and playhead indicators.
Typography: Use Barlow Condensed for timestamps, durations, and DAW metrics. Use DM Sans for UI text.
Wire all haptic feedback events (scrubbing, start/stop recording, tapping clips) according to the 'haptics' block.
